title: astragalus horridus
intro: Astragalus horridus is a plant species in the legume family, also known as the "milk vetch," which is native to the mountainous regions of Central Asia. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its adaptogenic properties, with medicinal components that include saponins, flavonoids, and polysaccharides. The health benefits of Astragalus horridus include its ability to boost the immune system, reduce inflammation, and improve cardiovascular health. Herbal preparations of Astragalus horridus include capsules, tablets, and teas, which are often used to support overall well-being and prevent illnesses.
